title: Easter Sunday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: March 31, 2024

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

Easter Sunday, the chief feast of the Christian year, celebrating the Resurrection of Jesus Christ. The lectionary readings would have been Acts 1:1-8 (or Isaiah 65:17-25), Psalm 118:1-2, 14-24, 1 Corinthians 15:1-11 (or Colossians 3:1-4), and John 20:19-23 (or Matthew 28:1-10) depending on whether the cathedral follows the Revised Common Lectionary or Book of Common Prayer. This marks the conclusion of the Lenten season and the beginning of Eastertide (the 50-day Easter season through Pentecost).

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in a period of relative stability following the 2022 General Convention, with ongoing discussions about pastoral guidance on blessings of same-sex unions being implemented in dioceses. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Kimberly Chastain (who had been serving since 2019), would have been focused on parish vitality and post-pandemic recovery. No major denominational crises or bishop elections were occurring in the immediate timeframe around this date.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing early spring weather typical for late March in Colorado, with temperatures beginning to warm after winter (highs around 55-65°F). The Colorado legislature was in its final weeks of the 2024 session (adjourning May 8), with various bills under debate. Saint John's Cathedral is located in downtown Denver's Capitol Hill neighborhood, an area experiencing ongoing revitalization and gentrification discussions. No major weather emergencies or local crises dominated the news in the week before Easter.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

President Biden's campaign was in full swing as the presumed Democratic nominee heading toward the 2024 general election. Former President Trump was facing multiple legal challenges, including the hush money trial in New York set to begin later in April. The Supreme Court was hearing arguments on major cases including abortion access and voting rights. March 2024 saw ongoing concerns about inflation and interest rates in economic reporting. College basketball's March Madness tournament was concluding, with the Final Four and championship game occurring during this week (NC State, Purdue, UConn, and Alabama in the final four).

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's war in Ukraine continued with active fighting and humanitarian concerns dominating coverage; March 2024 saw reports of continued Russian advances and attacks on infrastructure. The Middle East remained tense with ongoing Gaza conflict aftermath and regional instability. Taiwan held presidential elections on January 13, 2024, with results still being absorbed into foreign policy discussions.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in late March 2024 was in the midst of spring renewal, with Easter being celebrated across Christian denominations as a major cultural and religious event. March Madness enthusiasm was high, with sports dominating casual conversation. The broader cultural mood reflected ongoing polarization around the 2024 election cycle, with significant divisions in the American public. For an Episcopal congregation in Denver, the cultural moment combined traditional Easter celebration with awareness of the denomination's progressive social positioning on various issues, creating both unity around the Resurrection and underlying awareness of broader societal divisions.
